en: And while fleeing drivers are usually depicted in movies and TV shows as violent criminals,
ko: 영화와 TV에서 도주 운전자는 보통 폭력 범죄자로 묘사되지만 실제로는

en: in real life, that is rarely the case.
Researchers found roughly 90 percent of pursuits are initiated because of traffic violations.
ko: 거의 그렇지 않아. 연구에 따르면 추격의 약 90%가 교통 위반으로 시작돼.

en: And the reasons people tend to flee are often relatively mundane, from their license being suspended,
ko: 도주하는 이유도 보통 면허 정지나 보호관찰 위반이나

en: to being on probation, to simply saying they feared the police.
And this combination of high-stakes chases over low-stakes infractions
ko: 경찰이 무서워서라는 비교적 평범한 거야. 높은 위험의 추격과 낮은 위험의 위반이 합쳐져서

en: has resulted in situations like this, totally preventable tragedy in Georgia.
ko: 조지아에서 이런 완전히 예방 가능한 비극이 벌어졌어.

en: Larry Turner still can't believe his best friend of 30 years is gone.
I lost my brother. Sixty-year-old William Johnson was killed Sunday,
ko: 래리 터너는 아직도 30년 절친이 떠난 걸 못 믿어. 형제를 잃었어. 60세 윌리엄 존슨은 일요일

en: just before noon, as he waited on a red light at McAfee and Candler.
ko: 정오 직전 빨간 불에서 기다리다가 과속 SUV가

en: That's when this speeding SUV came out of nowhere and crashed into him.
ko: 갑자기 나타나 그에게 충돌했어.

en: Turner was floored when he learned a Georgia State trooper was chasing the driver of the SUV
ko: 터너는 주 경찰이 안전벨트 위반으로 SUV 운전자를 추격했다는 걸 알고 충격받았어.

en: for a seatbelt violation.
ko: 터너는 주 경찰이 안전벨트 위반으로 SUV 운전자를 추격했다는 걸 알고 충격받았어.

en: It's just not worth losing a life, or even being injured.
ko: 생명을 잃거나 다칠 가치가 없어요. 안전벨트 때문에. 맞아.

en: It's just not worth it. Over a seatbelt? Exactly, because that is absurd.
ko: 생명을 잃거나 다칠 가치가 없어요. 안전벨트 때문에. 맞아.

en: The whole point of a seatbelt law was to keep people safe on the road, so it's more than a little counterproductive
ko: 안전벨트법의 전체 요점이 도로에서 사람들을 안전하게 지키는 거잖아.

en: to then enforce it by turning the road into a fucking NASCAR track.
ko: 그걸 도로를 레이싱 트랙으로 만들어서 집행하면 좀 역효과야.

en: And that is not a one-off. Of the fatal chases The Chronicle was able to review in depth, over 1,500 people died
ko: 크로니클이 심층 검토할 수 있었던 치명적 추격 중 교통 위반, 비폭력 범죄,

en: in pursuits initiated over traffic infractions, non-violent crimes, or indeed, no crime at all.
In fact, people have died in chases that started with attempted police stops for things as petty
as having tinted windows, or a broken taillight.
ko: 틴팅이나 고장 난 후미등 같은 사소한 이유의 경찰 단속 시도에서

en: Which is just ridiculous, because someone who hasn't gotten around to fixing their taillight
doesn't deserve to be dangerously chased by the police.
ko: 사망한 사람도 있어. 터무니없어.

en: What they deserve is an ADHD diagnosis.
In fact, if anyone with a broken taillight is watching this show right now, I have an important message for you.
ko: 후미등을 고치지 않은 사람은 위험한 추격을 당할 자격이 없어. 그들이 받아야 할 건

en: You have clothes in the dryer, they've been in there for days, get them out.
ko: ADHD 진단이야. 혹시 후미등이 고장 난 채로 이 쇼를 보고 있다면

en: So, given all of that, tonight, let's talk about police chases.
ko: 중요한 메시지가 있어. 건조기에 빨래가 있어. 며칠째야. 꺼내.

en: Why they happen, and why the risks involved can massively outweigh the benefits.
ko: 그러니까 오늘 밤 경찰 추격에 대해 얘기해보자. 왜 일어나고
